On December 30, 1922, the incredibly massive, universally defining, and absolutely historic Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR) was officially and aggressively founded. Following the horrific, incredibly massive, and bloody carnage of the Russian Civil War, the massive First Congress of Soviets officially and and aggressively ratified the Treaty on the Creation of the USSR. This incredible, massive geopolitical maneuver fundamentally and and aggressively unified the massive, formerly independent Soviet republics into a single, incredibly powerful, and highly centralized communist superpower that would aggressively dominate the 20th century.

His incredibly massive, dramatic death fundamentally and and aggressively signaled the absolute, imminent collapse of the 300-year-old Romanov dynasty.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On December 30, 1906, the incredibly massive, historically definitive, and absolutely foundational All-India Muslim League was officially and aggressively founded in Dhaka (present-day Bangladesh). Heavily and and aggressively spearheaded by massive, highly influential leaders including the Aga Khan III and Nawab Viqar-ul-Mulk, this incredible political organization aggressively emerged as the absolute, primary vehicle for Indian Muslim interests. The incredibly massive institution fundamentally and and aggressively evolved into the absolute, driving force behind the massive Pakistan Movement, fiercely and eventually successfully demanding the absolute partition of the subcontinent in 1947.

On December 30, 1943, Subhas Chandra Bose, the incredibly massive, fiercely militant leader of the Indian National Army (INA), achieved an absolute, monumental historical milestone. In Port Blair, he officially and and aggressively hoisted the Indian national tricolor for the absolute very first time on massive, sovereign Indian soil. This incredible, massive act of defiance fundamentally and and aggressively established the absolute, administrative authority of the Azad Hind Government over the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, fiercely and and aggressively symbolising the incredibly massive, armed violent struggle to absolutely and and aggressively liberate India from the British Raj.
